response.justification: Bogdan has a broad and impressive software engineering background with genuine geospatial exposure (GISCollective) and high-performance distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby, but the role's core requirement — modern C++ or Rust for a routing data pipeline — is a significant mismatch. His C++ experience is isolated to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a student thesis project; there is no Rust experience whatsoever. His geospatial work is web-based GIS (Node.js/Ember.js), not routing tile generation or navigation pipeline engineering. His current trajectory is firmly in TypeScript, AI tooling, and agentic infrastructure, which is distant from the systems-level, latency-sensitive C++ work this role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective, an open-source event-driven geo-data platform with real-world usage
Proven high-performance distributed systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Strong Linux server background and comfort operating across diverse infrastructure stacks
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old; no modern C++ (C++17/20) and no Rust experience anywhere on the resume
No routing, tiling, or navigation pipeline experience — geospatial work is web GIS, not the routing data domain this role requires
Career trajectory for the last 6+ years is TypeScript/Node.js/AI tooling, showing a clear divergence from systems-level programming
